objects.name: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment
objects.description: Attackers use targeted phishing emails with malicious attachments (archives containing executable files, DLLs, LNK files, and decoy PDFs) to gain initial access.

objects.name: User Execution: Malicious File
objects.description: Victims are required to unpack an archive and execute an EXE or LNK file to initiate the compromise process.

objects.name: Component Object Model Hijacking
objects.description: Attackers use COM hijacking for persistence by registering malicious DLLs under specific CLSIDs in the Windows registry.

objects.name: DLL Side-Loading
objects.description: Attackers use DLL side-loading to execute malicious code by loading a malicious DLL through a legitimate executable.

objects.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
objects.description: Attackers obfuscate code and configuration data using techniques like Base36, Base64, RC4 encryption, and VMProtect packing.

objects.name: Application Layer Protocol: DNS
objects.description: TunnelRAT uses DNS tunneling for C2 communication, including custom domain generation and encoding.

objects.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
objects.description: Malware uses HTTPS and HTTP for C2 communication.

objects.name: Web Service: Bidirectional Communication
objects.description: EmojiRAT uses Telegram Bot API for bidirectional C2 communication.

objects.name: File and Directory Discovery
objects.description: Malware collects information about files and directories on the compromised host.

objects.name: System Information Discovery
objects.description: Malware collects system information such as domain name, computer name, username, and IP addresses.

objects.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
objects.description: TunnelRAT and EmojiRAT exfiltrate data over their C2 communication channels.

objects.name: Data Encoding: Non-Standard Encoding
objects.description: EmojiRAT encodes data using a custom emoji symbol table for C2 communication.

objects.name: Data Encoding: Standard Encoding
objects.description: TunnelRAT encodes data using Base36 and SSF client uses Base64 encoding for configuration data.

objects.name: Hide Artifacts: Hidden Files and Directories
objects.description: Attackers set the 'hidden' attribute for loader DLL files to evade detection.

objects.name: Hijack Execution Flow: DLL
objects.description: Attackers use DLL side-loading to hijack execution flow via legitimate executables.

objects.name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion: System Checks
objects.description: Loader and reconnaissance malware check for specific substrings in the USERDOMAIN environment variable to evade analysis.

objects.name: Indicator Removal: File Deletion
objects.description: Loader second stage checks for certain command-line arguments and deletes its own DLL file if found.

objects.name: Indicator Removal: Clear Persistence
objects.description: Loader second stage checks for command-line arguments and removes COM object registry values used for persistence.

objects.name: Dynamic Resolution: Domain Generation Algorithms
objects.description: TunnelRAT uses custom algorithms to generate domains for DNS-based C2 communication.

objects.name: Encrypted Channel: Asymmetric Cryptography
objects.description: Modified SSF client uses TLS for encrypted C2 channels.

objects.name: Proxy
objects.description: Modified SSF client enables SOCKS proxy functionality for attackers.

objects.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
objects.description: TunnelRAT and EmojiRAT are used to upload files to the compromised host.
